element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Two simultaneous shots; baseline weapon for learning controls
Completing formations yields power-ups; teaches target priority
Diagonal spread increases coverage; reward for formation destruction
Wide short-range spread; can destroy enemy fire; requires positioning
Single powerful line; requires accuracy; only weapon for continuous fire
Short burst per button press; alternative rhythm weapon
Alternates with all-aerial stages; introduces naval targets
Strategic bomber squadrons or single heavy bombers; requires engine targeting
Air-only; instantly destroys small air targets; resource management required
Surface-only; destroys air targets, damages ships, screen pause utility
Destroys all enemy fire; defensive option; critical for high-difficulty moments
Constantly depletes; refilled by power-ups; primary resource management mechanic
Shooting power-ups cycles between weapon types and items; teaches active item selection
Basic energy refill from red plane group completion
Enhanced healing; obtained by cycling POW power-ups

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Mid-late stages (special locations or rare drop)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Complete fuel restoration; rare; location-based or chance-based
Flanking support; weak and destructible; amplifies certain weapon spreads
Powerful piercing beam; obtained via beckoning cat; highest rarity

element_introduction_order.element: Star bonus (rare, weapon timer reset + 5000 points)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Rarer than Yashichi; strategic bonus; resets special weapon cooldown
Ultimate challenge; narrative climax; culmination of all learned mechanics

typical_relief_triggers: Destroying complete red plane formation (power-up drop)
Collecting POW or energy tank (fuel refill)
Screen pause from Tsunami attack (momentary safety window)
typical_tension_triggers: Dense enemy formations with no clear gaps
Enemy projectile saturation (Cyclone special use required)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Continuous resource pressure (fuel depletion) punctuated by relief moments from power-ups; alternating air-to-sea combat varies enemy patterns; escalating intensity across 16 stages with formation-based reward cycles creating micro-tension-relief loops

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Special hidden locations (Yashichi spawn points); player-driven exploration via shooting specific screen areas
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Shooting non-obvious targets triggers rare item drops (Yashichi, side fighters); bamboo shoots and bonus items appear via undocumented conditions

difficulty_curve.early_game: Learning weapon cycling and formation targeting; fuel meter introduces constant resource awareness; standard machine gun and basic enemy formations establish core loop
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Alternating air/sea stage types increase pattern variety; advanced weapons (Shell, Laser) introduce positioning precision requirements; boss difficulty increases; fuel management becomes critical
difficulty_curve.late_game: Stage 16 culminates with Yamato boss fight; heavy bombers require specific engine targeting; all weapon types and special attacks needed for optimization; sustained fuel efficiency essential
notable_spikes: Transition to all-aerial bomber stages (different attack patterns than sea combat)
Yamato final boss (narrative and mechanical climax)

notable_level_patterns.description: 11 stages feature sea combat with battleship/carrier bosses; 5 stages feature all-aerial combat with strategic bombers or heavy bombers. Alternating pattern creates rhythm variation and forces weapon/tactic adaptation

Destroying complete red plane formations guarantees power-up drops; cycling power-ups via shooting allows player agency in weapon/item selection. Creates micro-tension (complete formation) and relief (power-up choice) loop
Sea stages end with single large boss (battleship/carrier); air stages end with bomber squadron or single heavy bomber requiring engine targeting. Two distinct boss archetypes
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Stage 1 (first sea boss), Stage 2 or later (first aerial boss)

notable_level_patterns.description: Constant fuel depletion creates ongoing pressure; power-up collection provides relief windows. Fuel acts as invisible timer and resource gate, preventing indefinite camping or safe play
Yashichi and bonus items hidden in environment; player discovers via exploration and shooting non-obvious targets. Rewards curiosity and screen coverage

sources_used: wikipedia (Gameplay section: weapon types, items, special attacks, stage count, boss types, fuel system, formation mechanics)
tcrf (Regional differences and basic game information confirming 16 stages)
